title: atriplex laciniata subsp. elongata
intro: Atriplex laciniata subsp. elongata, also known as the orache, is a subspecies of the Atriplex laciniata plant, which is a type of shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Atriplex laciniata subsp. elongata include its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and help with wound healing, due to its rich nutrient profile. Atriplex laciniata subsp. elongata can be consumed as a tea, used as a tincture, or added to salads as a fresh herb, making it a versatile and valuable medicinal plant.
